questions.text: A user reports an email urging immediate payroll changes with a link to a look-alike domain. What should the SOC do FIRST?

options.text: Quarantine similar messages, block the domain at the gateway, and reset credentials if the user submitted data
Wait for three more reports before acting, using shared credentials instead of individual accountability.
Forward the email to personal Gmail for storage, using shared credentials instead of individual accountability.
Reply-all warning employees not to click, using shared credentials instead of individual accountability.

questions.explanation: Containment (quarantine/block) and credential safety come before broad communication.
questions.concept: Contain look-alike domains and user-reported phish; layer beyond link blocking (vishing, quishing).

questions.text: Your phishing simulation click rate dropped to zero after blocking all external links. What risk remains?

options.text: Voice phishing (vishing) and attachment-based malware bypass link filtering
Unsupported OS on printers only, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
ARP cache poisoning on Wi-Fi, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
No risk remains; email is fully secure, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Link blocking does not stop vishing or weaponized attachments—defense must be layered.
questions.concept: Contain look-alike domains and user-reported phish; layer beyond link blocking (vishing, quishing).

questions.text: An executive receives a QR code poster 'for IT Wi-Fi' in the parking garage. Which category does this BEST fit?

options.text: DNS tunneling exfiltration, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
SAST finding in CI, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Patch management failure, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Quishing (QR phishing) leading to credential harvesting

questions.explanation: Malicious QR codes are a growing phishing channel outside email filters.
questions.concept: Contain look-alike domains and user-reported phish; layer beyond link blocking (vishing, quishing).

questions.text: Users report a Teams message with a QR code linking to a fake O365 login. Best containment?

options.text: Disable Teams chat for all users until the next quarterly security training, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Forward the QR image to external researchers before taking internal action, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Remove messages org-wide, block the URL at proxy, and force password reset for submitters
Ask users to scan the QR with personal phones to verify if it is malicious

questions.explanation: Quishing bypasses email filters; rapid takedown and credential resets limit harm.
questions.concept: Contain look-alike domains and user-reported phish; layer beyond link blocking (vishing, quishing).

questions.text: Mail headers show SPF pass, DKIM fail, and DMARC none for a message impersonating the CEO. What improvement is MOST effective?

options.text: Allow any sender if subject contains 'urgent', without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Enforce DMARC p=reject with aligned DKIM for all executive domains
Disable SPF globally, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Remove DKIM to simplify troubleshooting, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: DMARC with alignment stops many spoofed From domains even when SPF alone passes via forwarders.
questions.concept: Email authenticity uses SPF/DKIM/DMARC alignment; display-name spoofing needs banners and verification.

questions.text: Users see internal display names matching a VP but the SMTP address is external. Which user-facing control helps?

options.text: Increase font size in Outlook, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Block PDF attachments from HR, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
External sender banners and training to verify the actual email address
Disable all calendar invites, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Display-name spoofing is common; banners and address verification reduce mistaken trust.
questions.concept: Email authenticity uses SPF/DKIM/DMARC alignment; display-name spoofing needs banners and verification.

questions.text: A VoIP caller ID shows your company's main number. What technical control reduces this spoofing vector?

options.text: Remove VLANs from the PBX, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Publish the main number on Instagram only
Disable MFA for call center staff, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
STIR/SHAKEN attestation on carrier trunks where supported

questions.explanation: STIR/SHAKEN improves caller ID integrity for voice spoofing on supported carriers.
questions.concept: Email authenticity uses SPF/DKIM/DMARC alignment; display-name spoofing needs banners and verification.

questions.text: DMARC aggregate reports show 12% of mail using your domain fails alignment from unknown IPs. Action?

options.text: Set DMARC to none permanently because reports are sufficient protection, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Allow all IPs to send as your domain to simplify vendor onboarding
Move DMARC policy toward quarantine/reject after fixing authorized senders and DKIM signing
Disable SPF because it conflicts with mailing lists and marketing tools, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: DMARC enforcement stops many spoofed messages once legitimate sources are aligned.
questions.concept: Email authenticity uses SPF/DKIM/DMARC alignment; display-name spoofing needs banners and verification.

questions.text: An invoice attachment is a password-protected ZIP containing a .lnk file. What is the SAFEST handling step?

options.text: Rename the .lnk to .txt to neutralize it, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Ask the sender for the password in email reply, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Sandbox detonation in an isolated environment before any user execution
Forward to finance to open on their desktop, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Password-protected archives bypass scanners; sandbox analysis avoids user execution risk.
questions.concept: Treat weaponized Office/PDF/archive attachments with sandboxing, not user execution.

questions.text: Macro-enabled Excel arrives from an unknown vendor address. Which policy is BEST?

options.text: Block macros from internet-origin files and use protected view
Allow macros if the spreadsheet has a logo, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable antivirus to speed opening, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Require users to enable editing on all attachments, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Office macros remain a common initial access path; blocking internet macros is baseline.
questions.concept: Treat weaponized Office/PDF/archive attachments with sandboxing, not user execution.

questions.text: Email filter quarantined a PDF with embedded JavaScript. Why is this noteworthy?

options.text: JavaScript in PDF only affects printers, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
PDFs can launch actions or download second-stage payloads when opened in vulnerable readers
Quarantine means the file is guaranteed safe, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
PDFs cannot contain active content, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Malicious PDF features are a documented attachment risk; quarantine is appropriate.
questions.concept: Treat weaponized Office/PDF/archive attachments with sandboxing, not user execution.

questions.text: An attachment is a .html file claiming to be an invoice preview. Safest handling?

options.text: Quarantine and detonate in sandbox; block HTML attachments at gateway if not business-required
Ask the sender to resend as a password-protected executable for integrity, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Rename to .txt so the mail client will not flag it as dangerous content
Open in the default browser on a finance workstation to verify the vendor name

questions.explanation: HTML attachments can phish or download payloads; sandbox before any user interaction.
questions.concept: Treat weaponized Office/PDF/archive attachments with sandboxing, not user execution.

questions.text: Finance receives wire instructions changing bank details via email right before quarter close. What verification is REQUIRED?

options.text: Out-of-band callback to a known number on file, not numbers in the email
Reply to the same thread asking 'Is this real?', without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Process if the logo matches, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Trust because the subject says 'CONFIDENTIAL', without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: BEC wire fraud relies on urgency; verified voice callback stops many losses.
questions.concept: Wire and payment changes require out-of-band verification and vendor domain controls.

questions.text: An attacker registers a domain one character off from your vendor. Which control reduces payment fraud?

options.text: Single approver if under $50k, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable accounts payable entirely, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Vendor master data with approved domains and dual approval on banking changes
Whitelist all .com domains, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Look-alike domains are classic BEC; vendor controls and dual approval are key.
questions.concept: Wire and payment changes require out-of-band verification and vendor domain controls.

questions.text: Legal notices a forged CEO email approving acquisition NDA sharing. Besides legal, who must be engaged FIRST?

options.text: Physical security for CCTV only, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Patch management team for WSUS, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Security operations to hunt for mailbox rules forwarding and similar threads
Facilities for parking passes, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: BEC often includes hidden inbox rules; SOC hunts prevent ongoing exfiltration.
questions.concept: Wire and payment changes require out-of-band verification and vendor domain controls.

questions.text: AP receives email changing ACH details for a top vendor; phone number in signature is new. Step?

options.text: Process the change if the email uses the vendor logo and familiar font styling, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Reply in-thread to confirm because the sender domain looks similar to the real one, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Forward the request to the CEO for one-click approval to meet quarter-end deadlines, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Call the vendor using the phone number in the ERP vendor master, not the email

questions.explanation: BEC payment changes must be verified out-of-band with trusted contact data.
questions.concept: Wire and payment changes require out-of-band verification and vendor domain controls.
